Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Complex The FLORAHOX Activity group aims to disrupt healthcare operations by gaining unauthorized access, moving laterally, and destroying sensitive data.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Shared DB or directory, Web server services such as emails and databases, Shadow IT, Poor implementation of access controls, Authentication issues
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Phishing, Vulnerability exploit, Brute-force attack, Credential compromise

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers gather information about the target healthcare organization's public-facing data, employees, and infrastructure.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attackers develop a convincing phishing campaign targeting specific employees within the organization.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2017-0001 [CWE-80]: Microsoft Office Memory Corruption Vulnerability
stage.description: The attackers initiate access by tricking an employee into opening a malicious attachment, exploiting a known vulnerability in their system.

Scenario.summary: The FLORAHOX Activity group employs a multi-stage attack strategy, starting with reconnaissance and resource development, followed by initial access via spear-phishing, execution of a backdoor, and persistence through scheduled tasks. They then escalate privileges, evade defenses, access credentials, discover network topology, move laterally, collect and compress data, exfiltrate it, and ultimately destroy sensitive data to cause impact.
